rewritten_content:
I ran into this exact issue and built a small macOS menu bar tool for it. It keeps multiple Codex accounts saved and switches the active one by writing `~/.codex/auth.json` atomically, plus shows remaining 5h/weekly usage per account.

Maintainer note: I build Codex Account Switcher.
Repo: https://github.com/naveenshaji/codex-account-switcher
rationale: More specific workflow detail, clearer value, and explicit maintainer disclosure.

rewritten_content:
If you want a developer-focused menu bar utility, I built one for Codex account switching. It lets you switch active account quickly, view 5h/weekly remaining usage, and launch a new CLI session after switching.

Maintainer note: I build Codex Account Switcher.
Repo: https://github.com/naveenshaji/codex-account-switcher
rationale: Matches the menu-bar-tools request and keeps tone practical without hype.

rewritten_content:
I had the same pain point, so I built a small macOS menu bar switcher for Codex accounts. It avoids manual auth-file edits and shows remaining 5h/weekly usage per account.
Maintainer note: I build it. Repo: https://github.com/naveenshaji/codex-account-switcher
rationale: Shorter X-friendly wording with concrete utility and disclosure.

rewritten_content:
For macOS, this is exactly what I built: Codex Account Switcher in the menu bar (multi-account switch + usage bars + quick CLI/session actions).
Maintainer note: I’m the maintainer. Repo: https://github.com/naveenshaji/codex-account-switcher
rationale: Direct response aligned to the ask with concise feature summary and disclosure.
